name: Kalmykia (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the sun and the Tibetan Buddhist faith of the Kalmyk people
the eternal blue sky and steadiness; the disc enclosing the lotus
the lotus flower, symbolising purity and spiritual rebirth

description: A golden-yellow field with a sky-blue disc in the centre, containing a white nine-petalled lotus flower.
history: Adopted on 30 July 1993, with regulations formalised on 8 February 1994. Replaced an earlier 1992 flag.

features.symbolism: yellow symbolises both the sun and the Tibetan Buddhist faith of the Kalmyk people.

features.role: white nine-petalled lotus on a sky-blue disc
features.symbolism: a white lotus inside a sky-blue circle, drawn from Buddhist iconography; symbolises purity, spiritual rebirth, and the emergence of enlightenment from adversity.
